new_classes.definition_en: A sparkling wine with a protected designation of origin (PDO), produced from grapes of the Glera vine variety harvested in one of the designated regions: 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', or 'Asolo — Prosecco'.
A quality borne by a sparkling wine indicating that it was produced exclusively from grapes of the Glera vine variety.

new_classes.label_en: Prosecco Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) quality
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a sparkling wine indicating that it carries one of the protected designations of origin: 'Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', or 'Asolo — Prosecco'.

coverage_explanation: The three core distinguishing criteria from the legal text are covered: (1) sparkling wine character (SparklingWineQuality), (2) exclusive use of Glera vine variety grapes (GleraVineVarietyQuality), and (3) PDO status within one of the four designated Prosecco regions (ProseccoProtectedDesignationOfOriginQuality). Disjointness with Champagne (the sibling PDO sparkling wine at 2204 10 11) is also expressed. A small gap remains: the four specific PDO region names ('Prosecco', 'Conegliano-Valdobbiadene — Prosecco', 'Colli Asolani — Prosecco', 'Asolo — Prosecco') are captured only in the definition of ProseccoProtectedDesignationOfOriginQuality rather than as individual named-region assertions, since no geographic region individual vocabulary for Italian PDO regions is present in the current TBox. This limits the coverage score to 0.92.
